responsibility_and_design_rationale: Validate schema, range, identifier, source identity, authentication, and freshness before data is eligible for trusted processing.
Apply configured rate-of-change, cross-signal, cell-spread, and redundant-source rules.
Update trusted state only after the complete required validation set passes; retain previous trusted state otherwise.
Separate validation from execution and require identity, authentication, freshness, authorization, and operating-state preconditions.
Enforce session, security, role, and operating-state matrices; ordered procedures; attempt counters; delay or lockout; and safe timeout behavior.
Verify signer, signature or hash, target, hardware or profile compatibility, security version, installation preconditions, and recovery state.

allocation: Gateway communication service and optional EMS
responsibility_and_design_rationale: Apply allowlists, timeouts, rate limits, bounded queues and stores, zone and role policy, and explicit protocol parsing.
Map loss of confidence to deterministic operating-mode-dependent degraded or safe actions from the safety-approved mapping; preserve cooling and evidence; and authorize recovery transitions.

allocation: Runner event sink and evidence store
responsibility_and_design_rationale: Record complete traceable results, isolate framework errors, bound event growth, and prohibit false PASS or finding closure without retest.

allocation: Secure-boot verifier and production HSM or secure-element concept
responsibility_and_design_rationale: Protect keys and provide hash, MAC, signature, and certificate operations. The lab uses vetted local cryptographic verification and disposable keys but does not reproduce production key management or a silicon root of trust.
